profile.name: Ronnie H. Legaspi
profile.hero_heading: Building reliable web and software applications with Laravel, React.js, PHP, and MySQL.
profile.hero_subheading: I'm Ronnie Legaspi, a BSIT graduate from the University of Eastern Pangasinan, eager to grow a career in IT and software development — turning ideas into working software, one commit at a time.
profile.about_paragraph_1: I build web and software applications using Laravel, React.js, PHP, MySQL, JavaScript, C#, and Unity. Proficient in database management, technical support, and Microsoft Office applications.
profile.about_paragraph_2: During my internship at AeonSprint Solutions Inc., I contributed to full-stack development, API integration, database management, and system maintenance on real production systems — and I'm eager to begin a professional career in IT, software development, web development, or administrative support, while continuously learning and contributing to organizational goals.

projects.description: HRFlow is a web-based HR automation platform that streamlines employee document requests, approval workflows, and automated document generation.

experience.title: Full Stack Developer (Intern)
bullets: Assisted in the development of web applications using Laravel and Vue.js/React.js, contributing to both frontend and backend development. Participated in API integration, database management, debugging, and system maintenance to support application functionality and performance.
Contributed to the development of an NFC-based attendance application using Laravel and React.js, with involvement in frontend and backend development, service integration, and database management.
Worked on web development, system optimization, database architecture, and UI/UX improvements.
Participated in the development of mini applications integrated into the main system (BadgerMint), contributing to feature implementation, testing, and system enhancement.
Co-hosted a webinar on "Bridging AI, Startups, and Secure Governance," facilitating discussions on emerging technologies, innovation, and cybersecurity best practices.

experience.title: Bachelor of Science in Information Technology
Core strengths
experience.description: Full-stack web development, database management, technical support, and Microsoft Office applications — with a continuous drive to learn and contribute to organizational goals.

posts.title: Department of Science and Technology 3 Day Even @ Dagupan City
posts.excerpt: Visiting the Department of Science and Technology (DOST) provided valuable insight into how science, technology, and innovation contribute to national development. It was an opportunity to learn about the agency's programs, research initiatives, and technological advancements that support education, industries, and communities.
posts.body: The visit also inspired a deeper appreciation for the role of technology in solving real-world problems and encouraged continuous learning and innovation in the field of Information Technology.

posts.title: Graduate as a Bachelor of Science in Information Technology
posts.excerpt: As a graduating Bachelor of Science in Information Technology (BSIT) student, visiting the Department of Science and Technology (DOST) was a meaningful and inspiring experience. It allowed me to see how technology, research, and innovation are applied to address real-world challenges and support national development.
posts.body: The visit broadened my understanding of the opportunities available for IT professionals and highlighted the importance of using technology to create practical solutions for society. It also motivated me to continue developing my skills and pursue a career that contributes to technological advancement and innovation in the Philippines.

posts.title: Building an NFC Attendance System with Laravel and React
posts.excerpt: Notes from my internship on wiring NFC hardware events into a Laravel API and a React front end for real-time attendance tracking.
posts.body:
During my internship at AeonSprint Solutions Inc., one of the projects I worked on was an NFC-based attendance application. The backend was built with Laravel, exposing a small set of REST endpoints that accepted tap events from the NFC reader and matched them against employee records in MySQL. The front end used React to show a live attendance feed as taps came in.

A few things I learned along the way: keeping the API contract between the reader service and Laravel dead simple made debugging hardware issues much easier, and using database transactions around each check-in/check-out pair avoided duplicate or inconsistent records when the reader occasionally double-fired. I also spent time on UI/UX polish so the live feed felt instant rather than laggy, which mattered a lot for a tool people would be looking at daily.

It was a great introduction to working across hardware, backend, and frontend in a single feature, and it's shaped how I think about designing APIs that need to stay reliable under real-world, imperfect input.
